title: Smoky Polish Pork Gratin
description: A hearty, comforting Polish-inspired dish featuring tender pork and a rich, smoky gratin.
instructions: Season the pork with salt and pepper.
In a large Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon of butter over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter to the pot, then add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually pour in the heavy cream, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the smoked paprika and grated cheese until melted and smooth.
Transfer the pork to a baking dish and spoon the gratin sauce over the top.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through and the top is golden brown.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
